hypothesis: kappa_nearest is causally sufficient: Delta_logit(q) = alpha_arch * Delta_kappa_nearest + epsilon, with epsilon arm-invariant across all intervention types
arms: j2_shift (m=1): move ONLY j2, j1 fixed (orthogonal to kappa_nearest)
top2_shift (m=2): move j1 AND j2 simultaneously
top_all_shift (m=K-1): move ALL K-1 competitors simultaneously

pass_criteria.arm_j2_r: r(Delta_j2_dist, Delta_logit_q) < 0.20 (orthogonal, neg control on kappa_nearest)
pass_criteria.epsilon_invariance: per-arm mean residual within +/-0.10 logit of zero

interpretation.j1_pass_j2_fail: kappa_nearest IS the causal variable (not just class pair distance)
interpretation.fixed_slope_r2_pass: kappa_nearest causally explains q with slope alpha_arch, regardless of which pair is moved

existing_scripts.reference_orthogonal: results/cti_orthogonal_factorial.json (arm_A r=0.899, arm_B r=0.450, arm_C r=0.000)
